q: DNA is often described as an instruction manual for the body. Which analogy best explains how DNA, genes, and chromosomes relate to each other?
o: DNA is a book, genes are letters, chromosomes are pages
DNA is the words, genes are sentences, chromosomes are paragraphs
DNA is the words, genes are sections of words, chromosomes are books
DNA is a chromosome, genes are books, chromosomes are libraries

q: Why do identical twins have slight genetic differences even though they come from the same fertilized egg?
o: Because they inherit DNA from different parents
Because environmental factors change their DNA sequences
Because mutations and gene expression can vary after fertilization
Because they have different numbers of chromosomes

q: Which of the following best explains why proteins have such diverse functions in the body?
o: Proteins are made of different types of cells
The sequence and folding of amino acids determine protein shape and function
All proteins have the same amino acid sequence but different sizes
Proteins are coded by multiple genes simultaneously

q: How does gene expression control the traits an organism shows?
o: By switching genes on or off to produce specific proteins as needed
By changing the DNA sequence to create new proteins
By increasing the number of chromosomes in a cell
By removing genes that are not needed from the genome

q: Which scenario best illustrates the influence of the environment on phenotype?
o: Two plants with identical genes grow to different heights because one receives more sunlight
Two animals with different genes have the same fur color
Identical twins have different blood types
A plant’s leaf shape is determined only by its genes

q: Continuous variation differs from discontinuous variation because:
o: Continuous variation involves traits with distinct categories, while discontinuous variation involves traits with a range of values
Continuous variation involves a range of values, while discontinuous variation involves distinct categories
Both involve distinct categories but differ in gene number
Both involve a range of values but differ in environmental influence

q: Why do multicellular organisms require transport systems, unlike unicellular organisms?
o: Because they have fewer cells that need nutrients
Because simple diffusion is insufficient over larger distances and more cells
Because they do not have cell membranes
Because they rely solely on osmosis for transport

q: In the human circulatory system, which blood vessels carry oxygen-poor blood back to the heart?
o: Arteries
Veins
Capillaries
Lymph vessels

q: Which of the following best describes active transport across a cell membrane?
o: Movement of particles from high to low concentration without energy
Movement of particles from low to high concentration using energy
Movement of water molecules through a semi-permeable membrane
Movement of particles through protein channels without energy

q: How does the structure of the DNA double helix facilitate its function in heredity?
o: The double helix allows DNA to be single-stranded for easy copying
The complementary base pairing allows DNA to replicate accurately
The twisted shape prevents DNA from interacting with proteins
The double helix structure stores proteins directly

q: Which of the following best explains why some traits, like height, show continuous variation?
o: They are controlled by a single gene with multiple alleles
They are influenced by many genes and environmental factors
They are inherited only from one parent
They are determined solely by the environment

q: Why is it important for cells to regulate which genes are turned on or off?
o: To ensure all proteins are made at all times
To produce proteins only when needed, conserving energy and resources
To increase the number of chromosomes in each cell
To change the DNA sequence for adaptation

q: Which of the following is NOT a correct pairing of a protein and its function?
o: Keratin - structural protein in hair and nails
Chlorophyll - protein that captures light energy in plants
Hemoglobin - protein that carries oxygen in blood
Insulin - protein that transports nutrients in blood

q: How do environmental factors affect skin color in humans?
o: Genes determine skin color completely, with no environmental influence
Sunlight exposure increases melanin production, darkening skin temporarily
Skin color changes permanently regardless of sun exposure
Skin color is only influenced by diet, not genes or sunlight

q: When graphing data for discontinuous variation, why is a bar graph more appropriate than a line graph?
o: Because discontinuous traits have distinct categories without intermediate values
Because discontinuous traits show a smooth range of values
Because bar graphs can show continuous data better
Because line graphs cannot display any biological data

q: Which of the following best explains why the circulatory system works closely with the respiratory and digestive systems?
o: To coordinate muscle movements for breathing and digestion
To transport oxygen and nutrients absorbed from food to cells throughout the body
To filter blood before it reaches the heart
To produce hormones that regulate breathing and digestion

q: In plants, why is the xylem essential for survival?
o: It transports sugars from leaves to roots
It transports water and minerals from roots to leaves
It stores nutrients for later use
It controls gas exchange through the stomata

q: How do Indigenous knowledge systems contribute to understanding environmental changes?
o: By relying solely on modern scientific instruments
By observing long-term natural patterns and using signs from plants, animals, and weather
By ignoring seasonal changes and focusing on human activities
By predicting environmental changes without any observational evidence

q: Why might traditional environmental indicators (tohu) become less reliable due to climate change?
o: Because climate change causes shifts in timing of natural events like flowering and animal migration
Because tohu are based only on human-made calendars
Because plants and animals no longer respond to environmental cues
Because climate change has no effect on ecosystems

q: How does the nitrogen cycle maintain ecosystem health, and what can happen if excess nitrogen accumulates in water bodies?
o: Nitrogen is removed permanently from ecosystems; excess nitrogen causes drought
Nitrogen cycles through organisms and soil; excess nitrogen causes eutrophication, harming aquatic life
Nitrogen is only stored in plants; excess nitrogen improves water quality
Nitrogen cycles only in the atmosphere; excess nitrogen has no effect on ecosystems

q: Which of the following best describes how mutations can affect an organism’s traits?
o: Mutations always improve an organism’s survival chances
Mutations change the DNA sequence and can be beneficial, harmful, or neutral
Mutations only occur in response to environmental changes
Mutations are changes in protein shape caused by temperature

q: How does sexual reproduction increase genetic diversity compared to asexual reproduction?
o: Sexual reproduction produces identical offspring
Sexual reproduction combines genes from two parents, creating new gene combinations
Sexual reproduction copies DNA without any changes
Sexual reproduction removes harmful mutations from the gene pool

q: Why is it important for ecosystems to have high biodiversity?
o: High biodiversity reduces competition among species
High biodiversity increases ecosystem stability and resilience to change
High biodiversity means fewer species are present
High biodiversity prevents any species from evolving

q: Which of the following best explains why some genetic disorders are more common in certain populations?
o: Because those populations have more mutations overall
Because of inherited genes that are more frequent due to historical mating patterns
Because environmental factors cause the disorders directly
Because those populations have fewer chromosomes

q: How can selective breeding in agriculture affect genetic diversity?
o: It increases genetic diversity by mixing many species
It decreases genetic diversity by choosing specific traits repeatedly
It has no effect on genetic diversity
It removes all harmful genes from crops

q: Which statement best explains the role of enzymes in biological processes?
o: Enzymes provide energy for reactions
Enzymes speed up chemical reactions without being consumed
Enzymes change the products of reactions permanently
Enzymes are only found in plants

q: How can the study of fossils help scientists understand organism diversity over time?
o: Fossils show the exact DNA sequence of ancient organisms
Fossils provide evidence of past life forms and evolutionary changes
Fossils prove that all species have remained unchanged
Fossils only show organisms that are still alive today

q: Why is the concept of “survival of the fittest” misleading when describing natural selection?
o: Because only the strongest organisms survive
Because fitness refers to reproductive success, not just strength
Because natural selection always leads to perfect organisms
Because organisms choose their traits consciously

q: Which of the following best explains how antibiotic resistance develops in bacteria?
o: Bacteria mutate randomly, and resistant strains survive antibiotic treatment and reproduce
Bacteria intentionally change their DNA to resist antibiotics
Antibiotics cause bacteria to become resistant immediately
Resistance is inherited from viruses, not bacteria

q: How do gene mutations contribute to evolution?
o: They provide new genetic variations that natural selection can act upon
They always cause harmful effects that reduce survival
They prevent organisms from reproducing
They remove existing genes from populations

q: How does genetic recombination during meiosis contribute to genetic diversity?
o: By producing identical copies of chromosomes
By swapping segments of DNA between homologous chromosomes
By doubling the number of chromosomes in gametes
By removing mutations from the DNA sequence

q: Which of the following best explains why some species are better adapted to survive in changing environments?
o: They have more offspring regardless of traits
They possess genetic variations that provide advantages under new conditions
They can change their DNA sequence at will
They avoid competition by migrating constantly

q: How does the structure of a cell membrane help regulate what enters and leaves the cell?
o: The membrane is rigid and allows all substances to pass freely
The membrane is selectively permeable, controlling passage of substances
The membrane is made of DNA and proteins that absorb nutrients
The membrane dissolves to let substances in and out

q: Why is meiosis important for sexual reproduction?
o: It produces cells with double the normal number of chromosomes
It creates genetically identical cells for growth
It reduces chromosome number by half to form gametes
It repairs damaged DNA in body cells

q: Which of the following best describes the role of RNA in protein synthesis?
o: RNA stores genetic information permanently
RNA carries the genetic code from DNA to ribosomes to make proteins
RNA builds the cell membrane
RNA breaks down proteins after use

q: How does natural selection act on variation within a population?
o: It eliminates all variations except one
It favors individuals with traits that increase survival and reproduction
It causes all individuals to change their traits simultaneously
It creates new traits by altering DNA sequences directly

q: Why might a species with low genetic diversity be at greater risk of extinction?
o: Because it can adapt quickly to environmental changes
Because it has fewer individuals overall
Because it has limited ability to adapt to new threats or conditions
Because it reproduces too rapidly

q: How can gene flow between populations affect genetic diversity?
o: It decreases diversity by isolating populations
It increases diversity by introducing new alleles
It stops reproduction between populations
It removes mutations from populations

q: Which of the following best explains why some traits are inherited in a Mendelian pattern?
o: Because they are controlled by multiple genes
Because they are controlled by a single gene with dominant and recessive alleles
Because they depend only on environmental factors
Because they are not influenced by genes at all

q: How does the concept of epigenetics challenge the idea that DNA sequence alone determines traits?
o: Epigenetics shows that environmental factors can turn genes on or off without changing DNA sequence
Epigenetics proves that DNA sequence changes frequently
Epigenetics states that all traits are inherited unchanged
Epigenetics removes the role of proteins in gene expression
